data.title: Jakob’s Law of Internet User Experience
data.description: Users spend most of their time on other apps, so they prefer your app to work similar to others they already use.
data.technical_spec: Intuitive touch gestures and simplified navigation resemble other popular applications.

data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: Simplifying choices reduces user decision time and boosts app usability.
data.technical_spec: Streamlined interface reducing complex tasks into simple actions.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Aesthetic designs are perceived as easier to use but require simplicity to achieve true usability.
data.technical_spec: Minimalistic UI with an emphasis on functional aesthetics.

data.title: Pareto Principle (80/20 Rule)
data.description: 80% of users use only 20% of the features, so prioritize what’s important.
data.technical_spec: Focus on optimizing critical features like messaging and calls.

data.title: Law of Proximity
data.description: Elements close to each other are perceived to be more related than those spaced farther apart.
data.technical_spec: Organized chat lists and grouped notifications for clarity.
